method: Bar chart sorted by enrollment count (y-axis) per course (x-axis), with color encoding to highlight top 10% (high) and bottom 10% (low) enrollment courses. Interactive tooltips show CName and exact counts.
objective: Identify courses with exceptionally high/low enrollment to prioritize further analysis

method: Faceted horizontal bar charts per Division (AS/EN). Each chart shows departments (DName labels) vs enrollment counts. Bars sorted descendingly, with search/filter widget (per I3) for direct department selection.
objective: Compare enrollment distribution across academic divisions and departments

method: Scatter plot with aggregated data: x-axis=course enrollment counts, y-axis=average gradepoint per course. Bubble size encodes credit value. Interactive brushing to select courses with high enrollment + high grades.
objective: Explore potential correlation between enrollment numbers and academic performance

method: Box plots showing enrollment distribution per faculty rank (Professor/AsstProf/etc.). Whiskers show min/max, boxes show 25-75 percentiles. Faculty names (Lname) visible on hover.
objective: Analyze if instructor rank influences course popularity

method: Heatmap with Days (rows) vs numerical Hours (columns), color intensity encoding enrollment counts. Adjacent histogram shows hourly distribution. Preserves raw Days values (per D3) for MW/ThF pattern recognition.
Bar chart showing % of enrolled students majoring in course's department (per D4). Overlaid line chart shows total enrollment. Interactive toggle between absolute/% views.
objective: Assess if departmental alignment with student majors drives enrollment
